name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَٰجِكَ إِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتَهَا فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا (٢٨)
```

```english
O Prophet! Say to your wives, ‘If you desire the life of the world and its glitter, come, I will provide for you and release you in a graceful manner. (28)
```

```arabic
وَإِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَٱلدَّارَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَـٰتِ مِنكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (٢٩)
```

```english
But if you desire Allah and His Apostle and the abode of the Hereafter, then Allah has indeed prepared a great reward for the virtuous among you.’ (29)
```

```arabic
يَـٰنِسَآءَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ مَن يَأْتِ مِنكُنَّ بِفَـٰحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ يُضَـٰعَفْ لَهَا ٱلْعَذَابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرًا (٣٠)
```

```english
O wives of the Prophet! Whoever of you commits a gross indecency, her punishment shall be doubled, and that is easy for Allah. (30)
```

```arabic
۞ وَمَن يَقْنُتْ مِنكُنَّ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا نُّؤْتِهَآ أَجْرَهَا مَرَّتَيْنِ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لَهَا رِزْقًا كَرِيمًا (٣١)
```

```english
But whoever of you is obedient to Allah and His Apostle and acts righteously, We shall give her a twofold reward, and We will have in store for her a noble provision. (31)
```

```arabic
يَـٰنِسَآءَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِّنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ إِنِ ٱتَّقَيْتُنَّ فَلَا تَخْضَعْنَ بِٱلْقَوْلِ فَيَطْمَعَ ٱلَّذِى فِى قَلْبِهِۦ مَرَضٌ وَقُلْنَ قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا (٣٢)
```

```english
O wives of the Prophet! You are not like other women: if you are wary [of Allah], do not be complaisant in your speech, lest he in whose heart is a sickness should aspire; speak honourable words. (32)
```

```arabic
وَقَرْنَ فِى بُيُوتِكُنَّ وَلَا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِيَّةِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ ۖ وَأَقِمْنَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتِينَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَطِعْنَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمُ ٱلرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ ٱلْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيرًا (٣٣)
```

```english
Stay in your houses and do not flaunt your finery like the former [days of pagan] ignorance. Maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and obey Allah and His Apostle. Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification. (33)
```

```arabic
وَٱذْكُرْنَ مَا يُتْلَىٰ فِى بُيُوتِكُنَّ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْحِكْمَةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ لَطِيفًا خَبِيرًا (٣٤)
```

```english
And remember what is recited in your homes of the signs of Allah and wisdom. Indeed Allah is all-attentive, all-aware. (34)
```

```arabic
إِنَّ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَٱلْمُسْلِمَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ وَٱلْقَـٰنِتِينَ وَٱلْقَـٰنِتَـٰتِ وَٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰدِقَـٰتِ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرَٰتِ وَٱلْخَـٰشِعِينَ وَٱلْخَـٰشِعَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ وَٱلْمُتَصَدِّقَـٰتِ وَٱلصَّـٰٓئِمِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰٓئِمَـٰتِ وَٱلْحَـٰفِظِينَ فُرُوجَهُمْ وَٱلْحَـٰفِظَـٰتِ وَٱلذَّٰكِرِينَ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَٱلذَّٰكِرَٰتِ أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةً وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (٣٥)
```

```english
Indeed the muslim men and the muslim women, the faithful men and the faithful women, the obedient men and the obedient women, the truthful men and the truthful women, the patient men and the patient women, the humble men and the humble women, the charitable men and the charitable women, the men who fast and the women who fast, the men who guard their private parts and the women who guard, the men who remember Allah greatly and the women who remember [Allah greatly]—Allah holds in store for them forgiveness and a great reward. (35)
```

## (Commentary)

These verses pertain to the wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). They first command him to inform them that they have nothing to expect from the world and its finery (زينتها) except for chastity (العفاف) and a bare sufficiency (الكفاف) if they choose to remain in the marriage of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). Secondly, the verses address the wives, stating that they stand in a difficult position despite its loftiness and honor; for if they are God-conscious (اتقين الله), they will be given their reward twice over, but if they commit a manifest indecency (فاحشة مبينة), the punishment will be doubled for them twofold. The verses further command them toward modesty and to remain in their houses without displaying their finery (تبرج), to maintain prayer (الصلاة) and give the zakat (الزكاة), and to remember the verses (الآيات) and wisdom (الحكمة) recited in their homes. Finally, the passage promises all righteous men and women, without exception, forgiveness and a great reward.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ** (O Prophet! Say to your wives...) [33:28] until the completion of the two verses. The context of these two verses suggests that the wives of the Prophet, or some of them, were dissatisfied with the narrowness and hardship of their livelihood in the Prophet’s (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) house. They complained to him about this and proposed that he make them happy in life by expanding their means and giving them of its finery.

Thus, Allah, the Glorified, commanded His Prophet to give them the choice between parting from him and receiving what they desired, or remaining with him under the existing conditions.

He presented their choice as being between "desiring the life of this world and its finery" and "desiring Allah, His Apostle, and the abode of the Hereafter." This choice indicates, firstly, that combining an expansive and luxurious life—attained through the enjoyment of the world and its finery—with being a wife of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and living in his house is something that cannot coexist.

Secondly, each side of the choice is restricted by the exclusion of the other. "Desiring the life of this world and its finery" means making it the primary objective (الأصل), whether the Hereafter is desired or not. "Desiring the Hereafter" means making it the primary objective in the heart’s attachment, whether the worldly life is expanded and its finery and luxury are attained, or none of that occurs.

Furthermore, the recompense—meaning the result of their choice of either side—is different. Should they choose the life of this world and its finery by parting from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), he is to divorce them and provide them all with worldly goods. However, should they choose to remain as wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), preferring the Hereafter over the life of this world and its finery, there is a great reward with Allah—but not unconditionally; rather, it is conditional upon being virtuous (الإحسان) and performing righteous deeds (العمل الصالح).

From this, it becomes clear that being a wife of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), in and of itself, carries no inherent nobility (كرامة) before Allah, the Glorified. Rather, nobility belongs to being his wife when accompanied by virtue and godwariness (التقوى). For this reason, when He later mentions their high status, He also conditions it upon godwariness, saying: **لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِنِ اتَّقَيْتُنَّ** (you are not like any other women, if you are God-conscious) [33:32]. This is similar to His saying regarding the Prophet and his companions: **مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللهِ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ أَشِدَّاءُ عَلَى الْكُفَّارِ رُحَماءُ بَيْنَهُمْ تَراهُمْ رُكَّعاً سُجَّداً** (Muhammad, the Apostle of Allah, and those who are with him are hard against the faithless and merciful amongst themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating...) until He says: **وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ مِنْهُمْ مَغْفِرَةً وَأَجْراً عَظِيماً** (Allah has promised those of them who have faith and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward) [48:29]. Here, He praised them generally based on their outward actions first, then restricted the promise of a great reward to those among them who possess faith and perform righteous deeds.

In short, the general application of His saying: **إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ أَتْقاكُمْ** (Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most God-conscious among you) [49:13] remains in effect, unviolated by any other form of nobility based on cause, lineage, or otherwise.

So, His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ** (O Prophet! Say to your wives...) is a command to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to convey these two verses to his wives. The implication is that he must divorce them and provide for them if they choose the first option, and keep them as his wives if they choose Allah, His Messenger, and the abode of the Hereafter.

His saying: **إِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا وَزِينَتَها** (If you desire the life of the world and its glitter...) [33:28]. Desiring the life of the world and its finery is a metonymy (كناية)—by virtue of the contrast—for choosing it, the heart’s attachment to its enjoyments, turning toward it, and turning away from the Hereafter.

His saying: **فَتَعالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَراحاً جَمِيلاً** (then come, I will provide for you and release you in a graceful manner) [33:28]. It is stated in *al-Kashshaf*: "The origin of **تعال** (come) is that it is said by someone in a high place to someone in a lower place; then its usage increased until it became equal across all locations. The meaning of 'come' (تعالين) here is: 'approach with your will and choice for one of the two matters.' It does not literally mean their physical rising, just as you say: 'He approached (أقبل) to dispute with me,' or 'He went (ذهب) to speak to me,' or 'He stood (قام) to threaten me.'" End quote.

Providing (التمتيع) refers to giving them wealth upon divorce to enjoy, and releasing (التسريح) is divorce (التطليق). A graceful release (السراح الجميل) is a divorce without animosity or quarreling between the spouses.

There are jurisprudential (فقيهة) discussions regarding this verse mentioned by the commentators. The truth is that the personal rulings it contains are specific to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), and there is no evidence from the wording itself that it extends to others. The detailed discussion belongs to jurisprudence (الفقه).

His saying: **وَإِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ** (But if you desire Allah and His Apostle and the abode of the Hereafter...) [33:29]. It was previously mentioned that the contrast between this sentence and the previous one restricts each by the absence of the other. Thus, the meaning of the sentence is: "If you desire and choose obedience to Allah and His Messenger and the felicity of the abode of the Hereafter, while being patient with the narrowness of livelihood and the deprivation of the world’s finery." This is also a metonymy for remaining in the marriage of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and being patient with the hardship of life; otherwise, the shared condition of virtue (الإحسان) for the promised reward would not be valid, as is evident.

So the meaning is: "If you desire and choose to remain in the marriage of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and be patient with the narrowness of life, then Allah has prepared for you a great reward, provided that you are virtuous (محسنات) in your deeds, in addition to your desire for Allah, His Messenger, and the abode of the Hereafter. For if you are not virtuous, you shall have nothing but the loss of both this world and the Hereafter."

**His saying, the Exalted**: **يا نِساءَ النَّبِيِّ مَنْ يَأْتِ مِنْكُنَّ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ يُضاعَفْ لَهَا الْعَذابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ** (O wives of the Prophet! Whoever of you commits a manifest indecency, the punishment shall be doubled for her twofold...) [33:30]. He turned from addressing the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) regarding them to addressing them directly to establish their obligation (التكليف) and for increased emphasis. This verse and the one following it are a confirmation and clarification of what was understood from His saying: **فَإِنَّ اللهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِناتِ مِنْكُنَّ أَجْراً عَظِيماً** (then Allah has indeed prepared for the virtuous among you a great reward), in both positive and negative terms.

His saying: **مَنْ يَأْتِ مِنْكُنَّ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ** (Whoever of you commits a manifest indecency). An indecency (الفاحشة) is an act reaching the extreme of repulsiveness and ugliness; it refers to major sins (الكبيرة) such as harming the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), slander (الافتراء), backbiting (الغيبة), and the like. "Manifest" (المبينة) means that which is apparent.

His saying: **يُضاعَفْ لَهَا الْعَذابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ** (the punishment shall be doubled for her twofold). That is, it shall be in the state of being twofold (ضعفين), and "twofold" means two equivalents (المثلان).

This meaning is supported by His saying regarding the side of reward afterward: **نُؤْتِها أَجْرَها مَرَّتَيْنِ** (We shall give her her reward twice over). Therefore, no weight should be given to the claim that doubling the punishment "twofold" means punishing them with three times the equivalent, based on the argument that doubling a punishment means increasing it, and if two equivalents are added to the punishment, the total becomes three times the original.

He concluded the verse with His saying: **وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً** (and that is easy for Allah) [33:30] to indicate that the nobility of being a wife or similar status does not prevent this, for there is no nobility except through godwariness (التقوى). Being a wife of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) only produces a beautiful effect when accompanied by godwariness; as for when it is accompanied by disobedience, it only increases one in distance and wretchedness (وبالا).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَمَنْ يَقْنُتْ مِنْكُنَّ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتَعملْ صالِحاً نُؤْتِها أَجْرَها مَرَّتَيْنِ** (But whoever of you is obedient to Allah and His Apostle and acts righteously, We shall give her her reward twice over...) [33:31]. Devout obedience (القنوت) is humility (الخضوع); it is also said to be obedience (الطاعة), or the persistence in obedience accompanied by humility. Preparation (الاعتاد) means to make ready, and the "noble provision" (الرزق الكريم) refers to Paradise (الجنة).

The meaning is: "Whoever among you is humble before Allah and His Messenger, or persists in obedience to Allah and His Messenger with humility, and performs righteous deeds, We shall give her her reward twice over—meaning twofold—and We have prepared for her a noble provision, which is Paradise."

The shift (الالتفات) from the third person to the first person plural in His saying: **نُؤْتِها** (We shall give her) and **أَعْتَدْنا** (We have prepared) is to signal proximity and honor, in contrast to the distance and disgrace understood from His saying: **يُضاعَفْ لَهَا الْعَذابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ** (the punishment shall be doubled for her twofold).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **يا نِساءَ النَّبِيِّ لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِنِ اتَّقَيْتُنَّ فَلا تَخْضَعْنَ بِالْقَوْلِ فَيَطْمَعَ الَّذِي فِي قَلْبِهِ مَرَضٌ** (O wives of the Prophet! You are not like any other women, if you are God-conscious. So do not be complaisant in your speech, lest he in whose heart is a sickness should aspire...) [33:32]. The verse denies their equality with other women if they are God-conscious, raising their status above others. It then mentions certain prohibitions and commands branching from the fact that they are not like other women, as indicated by His saying: **فَلا تَخْضَعْنَ بِالْقَوْلِ وَقَرْنَ وَلا تَبَرَّجْنَ** (So do not be complaisant in your speech... and stay... and do not display your finery...). These are qualities shared between the wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and other women.

Thus, beginning the speech with His saying: **لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِنِ اتَّقَيْتُنَّ** (You are not like any other women, if you are God-conscious) and then branching these shared obligations (التكاليف) from it, implies the emphasis (تأكد) of these obligations upon them. It is as if it were said: "You are not like others, so you must excel in complying with these obligations and be more cautious in the religion of Allah than other women."

The doubling of their recompense—both for good and evil—as indicated in the previous verse, supports and indeed proves the emphasis of their obligations, for the doubling of recompense is inseparable from the emphasis of the obligation.

His saying: **فَلا تَخْضَعْنَ بِالْقَوْلِ فَيَطْمَعَ الَّذِي فِي قَلْبِهِ مَرَضٌ** (So do not be complaisant in your speech, lest he in whose heart is a sickness should aspire). After explaining their high status and the loftiness of their rank due to their position with the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), and conditioning that upon godwariness—thereby clarifying that their virtue is through godwariness, not merely through connection to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)—He forbade them from "complaisance in speech" (الخضوع في القول). This refers to softening and thinning one's speech with men in a way that invites suspicion and stirs desire, causing "he in whose heart is a sickness" to aspire. This "sickness" is the lack of the strength of faith that would restrain him from inclining toward indecency (الفحشاء).

His saying: **وَقُلْنَ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً** (and speak honorable words). That is, speech that is proper and upright, recognized by the Sharia and Islamic custom (العرف الإسلامي). It is speech whose tone does not suggest more than its literal meaning, being devoid of any hint of corruption or suspicion.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ وَلا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ الْأُولى** (And stay in your houses and do not display your finery with the display of the former [days of] ignorance...) [33:33] until His saying: **وَأَطِعْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ** (and obey Allah and His Apostle). **قرن** (stay) is from *qarra yaqirru* when one remains firm; its origin is *iqrarna*, where one of the two 'r's was deleted. Or it is from *qāra yaqāru* when people gather. It is a metonymy for their remaining firm in their houses and adhering to them. Displaying finery (التبرج) is appearing before people like the appearance of towers (البروج) to onlookers. The "former ignorance" (الجاهلية الأولى) refers to the period of ignorance before the Prophetic mission (البعثة); thus, it means the ancient ignorance. The claims of some—that it refers to the eight hundred years between Adam and Noah (عليه السلام), or between Idris and Noah, or the time of David and Solomon, or the time of Abraham’s birth, or the interval between Jesus (عليه السلام) and Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)—are all claims for which there is no evidence.

His saying: **وَأَقِمْنَ الصَّلاةَ وَآتِينَ الزَّكاةَ وَأَطِعْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ** (and maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and obey Allah and His Apostle). This is a command to comply with religious orders. He singled out prayer (الصلاة) and zakat (الزكاة) for mention among them because they are the two pillars of worship (العبادات) and social dealings (المعاملات), then He encompassed everything in His saying: **وَأَطِعْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ** (and obey Allah and His Apostle).

**Obedience to Allah** (طاعة الله) is the compliance with His legislative obligations, and obedience to His Messenger is in what he commands and forbids by virtue of the authority (الولاية) established for him by Allah, as He said: **النَّبِيُّ أَوْلى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ** (The Prophet is closer to the faithful than their own souls) [33:6].

**His saying, the Exalted**: **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً** (Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification) [33:33]. The word "Indeed" (إِنَّما) indicates the restriction (حصر) of the Will (الإرادة) to the removal of impurity and purification. The term "People of the Household" (أهل البيت)—whether it is for the purpose of specific designation (الاختصاص), praise (مدحا), or address (نداء)—indicates the restriction of the removal of impurity and purification to those addressed by His saying: "from you" (عَنْكُمُ). Thus, in the verse, there are actually two restrictions: the restriction of the Will to the removal of impurity and purification, and the restriction of the removal of impurity and purification to the People of the Household.

The "People of the Household" does not refer to the wives of the Prophet specifically, because of the masculine plural address in His saying: "from you" (عَنْكُمُ) rather than the feminine "from you [all women]" (عنكن). Therefore, the address must either be for them and others—as it has been said that "People of the Household" refers to the people of the Sacred House (أهل البيت الحرام), who are the God-conscious, based on His saying: **إِنْ أَوْلِياؤُهُ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقُونَ** (its custodians are only the God-conscious) [8:34]; or the people of the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم); or the family of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), who are those conventionally considered his household, including his wives and relatives such as the family of Abbas, the family of Aqil, the family of Ja'far, and the family of Ali; or the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and his wives—and perhaps this is what is intended by what is attributed to Ikrimah and Urwah, that it is specifically for the wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم).

Or the address could be for others besides them, as it has been said that they are the relatives of the Prophet from the family of Abbas, the family of Aqil, the family of Ja'far, and the family of Ali.

In any case, if the "removal of impurity and purification" refers merely to religious godwariness (التقوى الديني) through avoiding prohibitions and complying with commands, then the meaning would be that Allah does not benefit from directing these obligations toward you, but rather He desires to remove impurity from you and purify you, similar to His saying: **ما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيَجْعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ حَرَجٍ وَلكِنْ يُرِيدُ لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ وَلِيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ** (Allah does not desire to put you to hardship, but He desires to purify you and to complete His blessing upon you) [5:6]. This meaning does not align with any of the previous definitions of "People of the Household," as it clearly contradicts the specific designation (الاختصاص) understood from the term, since it would then apply generally to all Muslims obligated by the rulings of the religion.

If the "removal of impurity and purification" refers to an intense and ultimate godwariness, and the meaning is: "This intensification of obligations directed toward you, O wives of the Prophet, and the doubling of reward and punishment, is not for Allah, the Glorified, to benefit from, but rather to remove impurity from you and purify you," and this were a generalization of the address to them and others after having specified them—then this meaning does not align with the address being specific to others besides them, which is obvious; nor does it align with the address being general to them and others, for others do not share with them the intensification of obligation and the doubling of reward and punishment.

It cannot be said: Why is it not possible for the address, on this assumption, to be directed toward them along with the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), whose obligation is intense like theirs?

Because it is said: He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) is supported by protection (عصمة) from Allah, which is a divine gift not acquired through action. Thus, there is no meaning in making the intensification of obligation and the doubling of recompense for him a preamble or a cause for the attainment of intense godwariness as a favor upon him, according to what the context of the verse provides. For this reason, no commentator has explicitly stated that the address is directed toward them with the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) only; we only entertained this possibility to validate the view of those who say the verse is specific to the wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم).

If the "removal of impurity and purification" refers to His Will, the Exalted, to do so absolutely—not through the direction of general obligation, nor through the direction of intense obligation, but rather an absolute Will to remove impurity and purify the People of the Household specifically as they are the People of the Household—then this meaning would contradict the conditioning of their nobility upon godwariness, whether the Will intended is the legislative Will (الإرادة التشريعية) or the creative Will (الإرادة التكوينية).

By what has preceded, that which has been narrated regarding the occasions of revelation (أسباب النزول) is supported: that the verse was revealed specifically concerning the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), Ali, Fatimah, and the two Hasans (peace be upon them), and no one else shares it with them.

These are numerous narrations exceeding seventy traditions (أحاديث). Those narrated through the chains of the Sunnis (أهل السنة) outnumber those narrated through the chains of the Shi'a. The Sunnis have narrated it through many paths from Umm Salamah, Aisha, Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, Sa'd, Wathilah ibn al-Asqa', Abu al-Hamra', Ibn Abbas, Thawban the client of the Prophet, Abdullah ibn Ja'far, Ali, and al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them), in nearly forty paths.

The Shi'a have narrated it from Ali, al-Sajjad, al-Baqir, al-Sadiq, and al-Rida (peace be upon them), as well as Umm Salamah, Abu Dharr, Abu Layla, Abu al-Aswad al-Du'ali, Amr ibn Maymun al-Awdi, and Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, in over thirty paths.

If it is said: The narrations only indicate the inclusion of Ali, Fatimah, and the two Hasans (peace be upon them) in the verse, and this does not contradict its inclusion of the wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), as suggested by the verse's occurrence within the context of addressing them.

We say: Many of these narrations—especially those narrated from Umm Salamah, in whose house the verse was revealed—explicitly state its exclusivity to them and its non-inclusion of the wives of the Prophet. The narrations will follow, including authentic (صحاح) ones.

If it is said: This is refuted by the text of the Book indicating their inclusion, such as the verse's occurrence within the context of addressing them.

We say: The entire matter rests upon the connection of the verse to the verses preceding it. These traditions, in their vast number, are explicit that the verse was revealed alone. Not even a single narration mentions that this verse was revealed as part of the verses concerning the wives of the Prophet, nor has anyone mentioned this, even those who argue for the verse's exclusivity to the wives of the Prophet, as attributed to Ikrimah and Urwah. Thus, the verse, in terms of its revelation (النزول), was not a part of the verses concerning the wives of the Prophet, nor connected to them. Rather, it was placed among them either by the command of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) or during the compilation after his passing. This is supported by the fact that the verse **وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ** (And stay in your houses) maintains its harmony and connection even if the Verse of Purification (آية التطهير) were assumed to be removed from between its sentences. The position of the Verse of Purification within the verse **وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ** is like the position of the verse **الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا** (Today the faithless have despaired...) [5:3] within the verse concerning prohibited foods in Surah al-Ma'idah. Discussion on this has already preceded in the fifth volume of this book.

Based on what has preceded, the term "People of the Household" (أهل البيت) becomes a specific name—in the parlance of the Quran—for these five: the Prophet, Ali, Fatimah, and the two Hasans (peace be upon them). It is not applied to anyone else, even if they were among his closest relatives, although it may be correct to apply it to them according to general custom (العرف العام).

Impurity (الرجس)—with a *kasrah* then a *sukun*—is an attribute derived from *al-rajasah*, which is filth (القذارة). Filth is a state in a thing that necessitates avoidance and aversion. It can be according to the outward appearance of a thing, such as the filth of a pig; the Exalted said: **أَوْ لَحْمَ خِنزِيرٍ فَإِنَّهُ رِجْسٌ** (...or the flesh of swine, for it is indeed an impurity) [6:145]. Or it can be according to its inward state—which is spiritual filth and impurity—such as polytheism (الشرك), disbelief (الكفر), and the effect of evil deeds. The Exalted said: **وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ فَزادَتْهُمْ رِجْساً إِلَى رِجْسِهِمْ وَماتُوا وَهُمْ كافِرُونَ** (But as for those in whose hearts is a sickness, it only adds defilement to their defilement, and they die while they are faithless) [9:125]. And He said: **وَمَنْ يُرِدْ أَنْ يُضِلَّهُ يَجْعَلْ صَدْرَهُ ضَيِّقاً حَرَجاً كَأَنَّما يَصَّعَّدُ فِي السَّماءِ كَذلِكَ يَجْعَلُ اللهُ الرِّجْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ** (...and whomever He desires to lead astray, He makes his breast narrow and straitened as if he were climbing into the sky. Thus does Allah lay defilement on those who do not have faith) [6:125].

Whatever it may be, it is a psychological perception and a conscious effect resulting from the heart’s attachment to false belief or evil action. The "removal of impurity" (إذهاب الرجس)—where the "al" (ال) denotes the genus—is the elimination of every vile state in the soul that errs from the truth of belief and action. Thus, it corresponds to divine protection (العصمة الإلهية), which is a psychological, cognitive form that preserves a human being from falsehood in belief and evil in action.

Furthermore, you have seen that the intention of godwariness or the intensification of obligations does not align with the exclusivity of the address in the verse to the People of the Household. You have also seen that such an intention does not suit the Prophet’s (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) station of protection (العصمة).

Therefore, it is necessary to interpret the "removal of impurity" in the verse as protection (العصمة). The meaning of "purification" in His saying: **وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً** (and purify you with a thorough purification)—which is emphasized by the verbal noun (المصدر)—is the removal of the trace of impurity by bringing its opposite after the removal of its root. It is well known that what opposes false belief is true belief; thus, their purification is their being equipped with the perception of truth in belief and action. The Will (الإرادة) intended here must also be other than the legislative Will, for as you have seen, the legislative Will—which is the direction of obligations toward the subject—does not suit the context at all.

The meaning is: Allah, the Glorified, continues His Will to specify you with the gift of protection (العصمة) by removing false belief and the trace of evil action from you, O People of the Household, and bringing upon you that which removes the trace of that, which is protection.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَاذْكُرْنَ ما يُتْلى فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ مِنْ آياتِ اللهِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ لَطِيفاً خَبِيراً** (And remember what is recited in your houses of the signs of Allah and wisdom. Indeed Allah is All-attentive, All-aware) [33:34]. The apparent context is that "remembering" (الذكر) here is the opposite of forgetting, as this is suitable for the context of emphasis and intensification found in the verses. Thus, it is like an exhortation after an exhortation to comply with the obligations directed toward them. In His saying: **فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ** (in your houses), there is another emphasis.

The meaning is: "And preserve what is recited in your houses of the signs of Allah and wisdom, and let it be in your minds so that you do not become heedless nor deviate from the path that has been laid out for you."

As for the claim of some—that the meaning is "And thank Allah for placing you in houses where the Quran and the Sunnah are recited"—it is far from the context, especially considering His saying at the end of the verse: **إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ لَطِيفاً خَبِيراً** (Indeed Allah is All-attentive, All-aware).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِماتِ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ** (Indeed the muslim men and the muslim women, the faithful men and the faithful women...) [33:35] until the end. Islam does not differentiate between men and women in being clothed with the honor of the religion. Allah, the Glorified, pointed to this generally in verses such as His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْناكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ وَأُنْثى وَجَعَلْناكُمْ شُعُوباً وَقَبائِلَ لِتَعارَفُوا إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ أَتْقاكُمْ** (O mankind! Indeed We created you from a male and a female, and made you nations and tribes that you may identify with one another. Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most God-conscious among you) [49:13]. Then He stated it explicitly in verses such as His saying: **أَنِّي لا أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عامِلٍ مِنْكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أو أُنْثى** (I do not waste the work of any worker among you, whether male or female) [3:195]. Then He stated it in detail in this verse.

His saying: **إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِماتِ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ** (Indeed the muslim men and the muslim women, the faithful men and the faithful women...). The contrast between Islam (الإسلام) and faith (الإيمان) implies a certain type of difference between them. That which informs the nature of their difference is the saying of the Exalted: **قالَتِ الْأَعْرابُ آمَنَّا قُلْ لَمْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَلكِنْ قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنا وَلَمَّا يَدْخُلِ الْإِيمانُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ** (The Bedouins say, ‘We have faith.’ Say, ‘You do not have faith; rather say, "We have submitted," for faith has not yet entered into your hearts’) [49:14] until He says: **إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَرْتابُوا وَجاهَدُوا بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ** (The faithful are only those who have faith in Allah and His Apostle and then do not doubt, and strive with their possessions and their persons in the way of Allah) [49:15]. This indicates, firstly, that Islam is the submission to the religion in terms of action and the outward limbs, while faith is a matter of the heart. Secondly, that faith—which is a matter of the heart—is a belief and internal conviction such that action by the limbs follows from it.

Thus, Islam is the practical submission to the religion by performing general obligations, and the "muslim men and muslim women" are those who submit to that. Faith is the heart’s commitment to the religion such that action by the limbs follows from it, and the "faithful men and faithful women" are those who have committed their hearts to the religion such that action by the limbs follows. Therefore, every believer (مؤمن) is a submitter (مسلم), but not vice versa.

His saying: **وَالْقانِتِينَ وَالْقانِتاتِ** (the obedient men and the obedient women). Devout obedience (القنوت), as has been said, is the persistence in obedience accompanied by humility. His saying: **وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالصَّادِقاتِ** (the truthful men and the truthful women). Truthfulness (الصدق) is the correspondence of what a person reports or displays with reality. Thus, they are truthful in their claim, truthful in their speech, and truthful in their promise.

His saying: **وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَالصَّابِراتِ** (the patient men and the patient women). They are characterized by patience (الصبر) during affliction and calamity, patience in obedience, and patience away from disobedience. His saying: **وَالْخاشِعِينَ وَالْخاشِعاتِ** (the humble men and the humble women). Humility (الخشوع) is an internal self-abasement of the heart, just as complaisance (الخضوع) is an outward self-abasement of the limbs.

His saying: **وَالْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ وَالْمُتَصَدِّقاتِ** (the charitable men and the charitable women). Charity (الصدقة) is the spending of wealth in the way of Allah, and it includes the obligatory zakat. His saying: **وَالصَّائِمِينَ وَالصَّائِماتِ** (the fasting men and the fasting women), through both obligatory and recommended fasts. His saying: **وَالْحافِظِينَ فُرُوجَهُمْ وَالْحافِظاتِ** (the men who guard their private parts and the women who guard [theirs]), which is by avoiding what Allah has not made lawful for them. His saying: **وَالذَّاكِرِينَ اللهَ كَثِيراً وَالذَّاكِراتِ** (the men who remember Allah greatly and the women who remember [Him greatly]). The object "Allah" is omitted in the second part because it is understood. They are those who frequently remember Allah with their tongues and their hearts, and this includes prayer and pilgrimage.

His saying: **أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةً وَأَجْراً عَظِيماً** (Allah has prepared for them forgiveness and a great reward) [33:35]. The use of the indefinite (التنكير) is for magnification (التعظيم).

## (Narrative Discussion)

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding the saying of the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ** (O Prophet! Say to your wives...). The reason for its revelation was that when the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) returned from the expedition of Khaybar—and had acquired the treasure of the family of Abu al-Huqayq—his wives said: "Give us what you have acquired." The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said to them: "I have divided it among the Muslims according to what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded."

They became angry at this and said: "Perhaps you think that if you divorce us, we will not find suitable matches from our people to marry us?"

Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, took offense on behalf of His Messenger and commanded him to withdraw from them. So the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) withdrew from them in the upper chamber of Umm Ibrahim for twenty-nine days, until they had menstruated and become pure. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed this verse, which is the Verse of Choice (آية التخيير). He said: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ** (O Prophet! Say to your wives...) until His saying: **أَجْراً عَظِيماً** (a great reward). Umm Salamah was the first to stand and said: "I have chosen Allah and His Messenger." Then they all stood and embraced him and said the same.

**I say**: Something similar has been narrated through the chains of the Sunnis, in which it is mentioned that the first of them to choose Allah and His Messenger was Aisha.

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain to Dawud ibn Sarhan from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): That Zaynab bint Jahsh said: "Does the Messenger of Allah think that if he lets us go, we will not find a husband other than him?" He had withdrawn from his wives for twenty-nine nights. When Zaynab said what she said, Allah sent Gabriel to Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: **قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ** (Say to your wives...) both verses. They then said: "Rather, we choose Allah, His Messenger, and the abode of the Hereafter."

In the same source, with its chain to Is ibn al-Qasim from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: I asked him about a man who gives his wife a choice, and she chooses herself—is she separated (بانت)? He said: "No. This was something specific to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) only; he was commanded with it and he did it. Had they chosen themselves, he would have divorced them, and that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ ـ إِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا وَزِينَتَها ، فَتَعالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَراحاً جَمِيلاً** (Say to your wives: 'If you desire the life of the world and its glitter, then come, I will provide for you and release you in a graceful manner')."

In *al-Majma'*, al-Wahidi narrated with a chain to Sa'id ibn Jubayr from Ibn Abbas, who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was sitting with Hafsah, and they quarreled. He said to her: "Would you like me to place a man between me and you?" She said: "Yes."

So he sent for Umar. When he entered upon them, he said to her: "Speak." She said: "O Messenger of Allah, speak and say nothing but the truth." Umar raised his hand and struck her face, then raised his hand and struck her face again.

The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said to him: "Desist." Umar said: "O enemy of Allah! The Prophet says nothing but the truth. By Him Who sent him with the truth, were it not for his presence, I would not have lowered my hand until you died." The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) then stood and went up to a room and stayed there for a month, not approaching any of his wives, eating his lunch and dinner there. Then Allah, the Exalted, revealed these verses.

In *al-Khisal*, from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) married fifteen women, consummated the marriage with thirteen of them, and passed away leaving nine. As for the two with whom he did not consummate the marriage, they were Amrah and Sana. As for the thirteen with whom he consummated the marriage, the first was Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, then Sawdah bint Zam'ah, then Umm Salamah—whose name was Hind bint Abi Umayyah—then Umm Abdillah Aisha bint Abi Bakr, then Hafsah bint Umar, then Zaynab bint Khuzaymah ibn al-Harith (the Mother of the Destitute), then Zaynab bint Jahsh, then Umm Habib Ramlah bint Abi Sufyan, then Maymunah bint al-Harith, then Zaynab bint Umays, then Juwayriyah bint al-Harith, then Safiyyah bint Huyayy ibn Akhtab, and the one who gifted herself to the Prophet, Khawlah bint Hakim al-Sulami.

He had two concubines (سريتان) for whom he would divide his time along with his wives: Mariyah the Copt and Rayhanah al-Khandafiyyah.

The nine he left behind were Aisha, Hafsah, Umm Salamah, Zaynab bint Jahsh, Maymunah bint al-Harith, Umm Habib bint Abi Sufyan, Juwayriyah, Sawdah, and Safiyyah. The best of them was Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, then Umm Salamah, then Maymunah.

In *al-Majma'*, regarding His saying: **يا نِساءَ النَّبِيِّ مَنْ يَأْتِ مِنْكُنَّ** (O wives of the Prophet! Whoever of you commits...) the two verses: Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ibrahim ibn Abd al-Hamid from Ali ibn Abdillah ibn al-Husayn from his father from Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him): That a man said: "You are a Household (أهل بيت) who have been forgiven." He (peace be upon him) became angry and said: "We are more worthy of having that which Allah applied to the wives of the Prophet applied to us than being as you say. We see for the virtuous among us twice the reward, and for the evildoer among us twice the punishment."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, narrated from Abu Abdillah from his father (peace be upon him): Regarding this verse **وَلا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ الْأُولى** (and do not display your finery with the display of the former [days of] ignorance), he said: "Meaning, there will be a second ignorance."

**I say**: This is a subtle derivation.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, al-Tabarani extracted from Umm Salamah that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said to Fatimah: "Bring me your husband and your two sons." She brought them, and the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) cast a Fadaki cloak over them. Then he placed his hand upon them and said: **اللهم إن هؤلاء أهل محمد ـ وفي لفظ آل محمد ـ فاجعل صلواتك وبركاتك على آل محمد ـ كما جعلتها على آل إبراهيم إنك حميد مجيد** — "O Allah, these are the People of Muhammad—and in another wording, the Family of Muhammad—so place Your blessings and Your benedictions upon the Family of Muhammad, just as You placed them upon the Family of Abraham; indeed You are All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious."

Umm Salamah said: "I lifted the cloak to enter with them, but he pulled it from my hand and said: 'Indeed, you are upon good.'"

**I say**: It was also narrated in *Ghayat al-Maram* from Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal from his father with his chain to Umm Salamah.

In the same source, Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Umm Salamah, who said: This verse was revealed in my house: **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً** (Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification). In the house were seven: Gabriel, Michael, Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and I was at the door of the house. I said: "O Messenger of Allah, am I not of the People of the Household?" He said: "Indeed, you are upon good; you are of the wives of the Prophet."

In the same source, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Tabarani, and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Umm Salamah, the wife of the Prophet: That the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was in her house on a couch of his, covered by a Khaybari cloak. Fatimah came with a stone pot containing a meat dish (خزيرة). The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Call your husband and your two sons, Hasan and Husayn." She called them, and while they were eating, there was revealed to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً**.

The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) took the excess of his cloak and covered them with it. Then he brought his hand out from the cloak and pointed it toward the sky, then said: **اللهم هؤلاء أهل بيتي وخاصتي ـ فأذهب عنهم الرجس وطهرهم تطهيرا** — "O Allah, these are the People of my Household and my elite; so remove impurity from them and purify them with a thorough purification." He said it three times.

Umm Salamah said: "I put my head into the curtain and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, and am I with you?' He said: 'Indeed, you are toward good,' twice."

**I say**: The tradition was narrated in *Ghayat al-Maram* from Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal through three paths from Umm Salamah, and likewise from the *Tafsir* of al-Tha'labi.

In the same source, Ibn Mardawayh and al-Khatib extracted from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, who said: It was the day of Umm Salamah, the Mother of the Believers, when Gabriel descended to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) with this verse: **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً**. He said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) called for Hasan, Husayn, Fatimah, and Ali, drew them to himself, and spread the garment over them, while the veil was drawn over Umm Salamah. Then he said: **اللهم هؤلاء أهل بيتي ـ اللهم أذهب عنهم الرجس وطهرهم تطهيرا** — "O Allah, these are the People of my Household; O Allah, remove impurity from them and purify them with a thorough purification." Umm Salamah said: "And am I with them, O Prophet of Allah?" He said: "You are in your place, and indeed you are upon good."

In the same source, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and al-Tabarani extracted from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "This verse was revealed concerning five: concerning me, Ali, Fatimah, Hasan, and Husayn—**إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً**."

**I say**: It was also narrated in *Ghayat al-Maram* from al-Tha'labi in his *Tafsir*.

In the same source, al-Tirmidhi extracted and authenticated it, as did Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, al-Hakim (who authenticated it), Ibn Mardawayh, and al-Bayhaqi in his *Sunan* through paths from Umm Salamah, who said: In my house was revealed: **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ**. In the house were Fatimah, Ali, Hasan, and Husayn. The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) covered them with a cloak he had on, then said: **هؤلاء أهل بيتي ـ فأذهب عنهم الرجس وطهرهم تطهيرا** — "These are the People of my Household; so remove impurity from them and purify them with a thorough purification."

In *Ghayat al-Maram*, from al-Humaydi, who said: The sixty-fourth of what is agreed upon in the two *Sahihs* from al-Bukhari and Muslim, from the *Musnad* of Aisha, from Mus'ab ibn Shaybah from Safiyyah bint Shaybah from Aisha, who said: The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) went out one morning wearing a patterned cloak (مرط مرحل) of black hair. Hasan ibn Ali came and he entered him [under it], then Husayn came and he entered him with him, then Fatimah came and he entered her, then Ali came and he entered him. Then he said: **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً**.

**I say**: The tradition is narrated from her through various paths.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, who said: When Ali went in unto Fatimah [after marriage], the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) came for forty mornings to her door, saying: **السلام عليكم أهل البيت ورحمة الله وبركاته ـ الصلاة رحمكم الله ـ إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً ـ أنا حرب لمن حاربتم أنا سلم لمن سالمتم** — "Peace be upon you, O People of the Household, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. Prayer! May Allah have mercy on you. 'Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification.' I am at war with those you war with, and I am at peace with those you are at peace with."

In the same source, Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Ibn Abbas, who said: We witnessed the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) for nine months coming every day to the door of Ali ibn Abi Talib at the time of every prayer, saying: **السلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته أهل البيت إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً** — "Peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah and His blessings, O People of the Household. 'Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification.'" **I say**: It was also narrated from al-Tabarani from Abu al-Hamra', and his wording is: "I saw the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) coming to the door of Ali and Fatimah for six months, saying: 'Indeed Allah desires...'" the verse.

Also from Ibn Jarir and Ibn Mardawayh from Abu al-Hamra', and his wording is: "I memorized from the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) for eight months in Medina; not once did he go out to the morning prayer except that he came to the door of Ali, placed his hand on the two sides of the door, and then said: 'Prayer! Prayer! Indeed Allah desires to repel...'" the verse.

It was also narrated from Ibn Abi Shaybah, Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi (who graded it fair), Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, al-Tabarani, al-Hakim (who authenticated it), and Ibn Mardawayh from Anas, and his wording is: That the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) used to pass by the door of Fatimah when he went out to the dawn prayer and say: **الصلاة يا أهل البيت الصلاة ـ إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ـ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً** — "Prayer, O People of the Household, prayer! 'Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification.'"

**I say**: The narrations on these meanings from the paths of the Sunnis are many, and likewise from the paths of the Shi'a. Whoever wishes to examine them should refer to *Ghayat al-Maram* by al-Bahrani and *al-Abaqat*.

In *Ghayat al-Maram*, from al-Hamawini with his chain to Yazid ibn Hayyan, who said: We entered upon Zayd ibn Arqam and he said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) addressed us and said: **ألا إني تركت فيكم الثقلين ـ أحدهما كتاب الله عز وجل ـ من اتبعه كان على هدى ومن تركه كان على ضلالة ، ثم أهل بيتي أذكركم الله في أهل بيتي ثلاث مرات** — "Behold, I have left among you the two weighty things (الثقلين): one of them is the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic—whoever follows it is upon guidance and whoever leaves it is upon error; then the People of my Household. I remind you of Allah regarding the People of my Household," three times.

We said: "Are his wives of the People of his Household?" He said: "No. The People of his Household are his close kin (عصبته) who were forbidden from receiving charity (الصدقة) after him: the family of Ali, the family of Abbas, the family of Ja'far, and the family of Aqil."

In the same source, also from Muslim in his *Sahih* with his chain to Yazid ibn Hayyan from Zayd ibn Arqam, who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: **إني تارك فيكم الثقلين ـ أحدهما كتاب الله هو حبل الله ـ من اتبعه كان على الهدى ومن تركه كان على ضلالة** — "I am leaving among you the two weighty things: one of them is the Book of Allah, which is the rope of Allah—whoever follows it is upon guidance and whoever leaves it is upon error." We said: "Are his wives of the People of his Household?" He said: "No, by Allah! A woman may be with a man for a period of time, then for an age, then he divorces her and she returns to her family and her people. The People of his Household are his root and his close kin who were forbidden from receiving charity after him."

**I say**: He interpreted "Household" (البيت) as lineage (النسب), as this meaning is used conventionally; it is said "the households of the Arabs" meaning their lineages. However, the previous narrations from Umm Salamah and others refute this meaning and the interpretation of "People of the Household" as Ali, Fatimah, and their two sons (peace be upon them).

In *al-Majma'*, Muqatil ibn Hayyan said: When Asma bint Umays returned from Abyssinia with her husband Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, she entered upon the wives of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: "Has anything of the Quran been revealed concerning us?" They said: "No."

So she came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, indeed women are in disappointment and loss." He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "And why is that?" She said: "Because they are not mentioned with good as men are mentioned." Then Allah, the Exalted, revealed this verse: **إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِماتِ** (Indeed the muslim men and the muslim women...) until the end.

**I say**: In other narrations, it is mentioned that the one who said this was Umm Salamah.
